skills.name: code-review
skills.description: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level. Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply them.
skills.when_to_use: When asked to review code changes, a PR, or a branch for bugs and quality issues.

skills.name: simplify
skills.description: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — does not hunt for bugs.
skills.when_to_use: After implementing a feature, to clean up the code without changing behavior.

skills.name: verify
skills.description: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior.
skills.when_to_use: When asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, or validate local changes before pushing.

skills.name: security-review
skills.description: Run a structured security audit on the current changes, identifying OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities, secrets exposure, and authentication flaws.
skills.when_to_use: Before merging changes that touch auth, API endpoints, user data handling, or dependency updates.

skills.name: deep-research
skills.description: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report.
skills.when_to_use: When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic.

skills.name: loop
skills.description: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval. Omit the interval to let the model self-pace.
skills.when_to_use: When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval.

skills.name: schedule
skills.description: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule.
skills.when_to_use: When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, or manage their scheduled agents.

skills.name: run
skills.description: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working.
skills.when_to_use: When asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests).

skills.name: init
skills.description: Initialize project — set up Claude Code configuration, CLAUDE.md, and recommended settings for a new codebase.
skills.when_to_use: When starting work in a new repository or onboarding onto an existing codebase.

skills.name: create-prompt
skills.description: Create a well-structured prompt for a specific task or use case.
skills.when_to_use: When the user wants to create a reusable prompt template for a recurring workflow.

skills.name: research-brief
skills.description: Produce a concise research brief on a topic — key facts, trade-offs, and recommended next steps.
skills.when_to_use: When the user needs a quick orientation on a new technology, library, or design decision.
